body.value: 草高 三拾六萬九千四百拾壹石餘
草高 (くさだか) 三十六万九千四百十一石余

Sōdaka = 369,411 koku. This is the base assessed kokudaka before new-field additions. Below the formal title sōdaka of 369k, the actual Chōshū domain held substantially more land via new-field development (see 外高 column to the left).
草高
外 高 六拾貳萬八千五百九十三石餘 (新田 打出)
外高 (新田・打出) 六十二万八千五百九十三石余

External 'high' (new-field / uchikiage uplift) = 628,593 koku. Land developed after the original kokudaka assessment — primarily reclamation and intensification work over the Edo period. This is dramatically larger than the base 草高.
外 高
合 高 九拾八萬八千四石餘
合高 (ごうだか) 九十八万八千四石余

Combined high (godaka) = 988,004 koku. **INTERNAL HI ARITHMETIC NOTE**: 369,411 + 628,593 = 998,004, NOT 988,004. The HI source itself has a 10,000-koku arithmetic inconsistency on this page (likely a printing error in the 1870 edition). MASTER captures the source faithfully — both the components and the printed-but-wrong total. The dataset's kokudaka_iv uses the 988,004 combined figure as printed by HI.
合高
正租 現米 貳拾萬六千九百拾石
正租 (せいそ) 現米 二十万六千九百十石

Main tax rice (seiso genmai) = 206,910 koku. The actual flow of rice to the domain treasury. About 21% of the assessed 合高 (988k), broadly consistent with Edo-period effective tax rates of 20-30% on rice production.
正租 現米
